curriculum.math: Common Core State Standards for Mathematics (CCSSM) K-8

metadata.note: Chemistry topics appear from grade 2 onward per NGSS; full chemistry strand begins grade 5. Earth Science woven into science strand throughout. Topics include standard_id (CCSSM or NGSS code), unlock dependencies, and recommended question formats.

grade_1.name: Addition word problems within 20
grade_1.description: Solve addition word problems using objects, drawings, and equations
grade_1.subject: math

grade_1.name: Subtraction word problems within 20
grade_1.description: Solve subtraction word problems using objects, drawings, and equations
grade_1.subject: math

grade_1.name: Properties of addition
grade_1.description: Apply commutative and associative properties of addition
grade_1.subject: math

grade_1.name: Addition and subtraction fluency within 10
grade_1.description: Add and subtract within 10 using mental strategies
grade_1.subject: math

grade_1.name: Count to 120
grade_1.description: Count, read, and write numerals to 120; understand place in sequence
grade_1.subject: math

grade_1.name: Tens and ones (place value)
grade_1.description: Understand two-digit numbers as bundles of tens and ones
grade_1.subject: math

grade_1.name: Adding within 100
grade_1.description: Add within 100 using place value strategies
grade_1.subject: math

grade_1.name: Telling time to the hour and half-hour
grade_1.description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks
grade_1.subject: math

grade_1.name: 2D and 3D shapes
grade_1.description: Distinguish between defining attributes of shapes; build and draw shapes
grade_1.subject: math

grade_2.name: Addition and subtraction word problems within 100
grade_2.description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems
grade_2.subject: math

grade_2.name: Fluency with addition and subtraction within 20
grade_2.description: Fluently add and subtract within 20 using mental strategies
grade_2.subject: math

grade_2.name: Even and odd numbers
grade_2.description: Determine whether a group of objects is odd or even; write equations for even numbers
grade_2.subject: math

grade_2.name: Hundreds, tens, and ones
grade_2.description: Understand three-digit numbers as hundreds, tens, and ones
grade_2.subject: math

grade_2.name: Introduction to arrays and repeated addition
grade_2.description: Use addition to find the total number of objects in rectangular arrays
grade_2.subject: math

grade_2.name: Add and subtract within 1000
grade_2.description: Add and subtract within 1000 using place value and properties
grade_2.subject: math

grade_2.name: Measuring length
grade_2.description: Measure the length of an object using appropriate tools
grade_2.subject: math

grade_2.name: Telling time to the nearest 5 minutes
grade_2.description: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes
grade_2.subject: math

grade_2.name: Shapes and their attributes
grade_2.description: Recognize and draw shapes with specific attributes; partition shapes
grade_2.subject: math

grade_3.name: Multiplication concepts
grade_3.description: Interpret multiplication as equal groups; represent with equations
grade_3.subject: math

grade_3.name: Division concepts
grade_3.description: Interpret division as equal sharing or grouping; relate to multiplication
grade_3.subject: math

grade_3.name: Properties of multiplication
grade_3.description: Apply commutative, associative, and distributive properties
grade_3.subject: math

grade_3.name: Multiplication and division fluency within 100
grade_3.description: Fluently multiply and divide within 100; know all products of one-digit numbers
grade_3.subject: math

grade_3.name: Two-step word problems
grade_3.description: Solve two-step word problems using four operations; assess reasonableness
grade_3.subject: math

grade_3.name: Add and subtract within 1000 fluently
grade_3.description: Fluently add and subtract within 1000 using algorithms based on place value
grade_3.subject: math

grade_3.name: Introduction to fractions
grade_3.description: Understand fractions as parts of a whole; unit fractions on number line
grade_3.subject: math

grade_3.name: Time and elapsed time
grade_3.description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute; solve elapsed time problems
grade_3.subject: math

grade_3.name: Quadrilaterals and shape categories
grade_3.description: Understand categories of shapes; reason about shared attributes
grade_3.subject: math

grade_4.name: Multiplicative comparisons
grade_4.description: Interpret multiplication equations as comparisons; solve comparison word problems
grade_4.subject: math

grade_4.name: Factors, multiples, primes, and composites
grade_4.description: Find factor pairs; identify prime and composite numbers 1–100
grade_4.subject: math

grade_4.name: Number patterns
grade_4.description: Generate and analyze patterns; identify features not explicit in the rule
grade_4.subject: math

grade_4.name: Equivalent fractions
grade_4.description: Explain equivalent fractions using visual models; generate equivalent fractions
grade_4.subject: math

grade_4.name: Add and subtract fractions
grade_4.description: Add and subtract mixed numbers and fractions with like denominators
grade_4.subject: math

grade_4.name: Multiply fractions by whole numbers
grade_4.description: Understand fraction as multiple of unit fraction; multiply fractions by whole numbers
grade_4.subject: math

grade_4.name: Decimal notation for fractions
grade_4.description: Use decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100
grade_4.subject: math

grade_4.name: Measurement conversions
grade_4.description: Know relative sizes of units; convert larger to smaller units within a system
grade_4.subject: math

grade_4.name: Lines, angles, and rays
grade_4.description: Draw and identify points, lines, line segments, rays, and angles
grade_4.subject: math

grade_5.name: Order of operations
grade_5.description: Use parentheses, brackets, braces; evaluate expressions with these symbols
grade_5.subject: math

grade_5.name: Numerical patterns and graphing
grade_5.description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules; graph on coordinate plane
grade_5.subject: math

grade_5.name: Decimals to thousandths
grade_5.description: Read, write, compare decimals to thousandths; understand place value system
grade_5.subject: math

grade_5.name: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators
grade_5.description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators using equivalent fractions
grade_5.subject: math

grade_5.name: Multiply fractions
grade_5.description: Multiply fractions and mixed numbers; interpret multiplication as scaling
grade_5.subject: math

grade_5.name: Divide fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by fractions
grade_5.description: Apply understanding of division to divide unit fractions
grade_5.subject: math

grade_5.name: Multi-digit multiplication
grade_5.description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using standard algorithm
grade_5.subject: math

grade_5.name: Multi-digit division
grade_5.description: Find whole-number quotients with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors
grade_5.subject: math

grade_5.name: Coordinate plane
grade_5.description: Use a coordinate system; graph points in the first quadrant
grade_5.subject: math

grade_6.name: Ratios and unit rates
grade_6.description: Understand ratio concepts; use ratio language to describe relationships
grade_6.subject: math

grade_6.name: Ratios, percentages, and proportional reasoning
grade_6.description: Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve problems including percent
grade_6.subject: math

grade_6.name: Negative numbers and the number line
grade_6.description: Understand positive and negative numbers; use on number line and coordinate plane
grade_6.subject: math

grade_6.name: Operations with decimals
grade_6.description: Fluently add, subtract, multiply, divide multi-digit decimals
grade_6.subject: math

grade_6.name: Expressions with exponents
grade_6.description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents
grade_6.subject: math

grade_6.name: Algebraic expressions
grade_6.description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions with variables; identify parts of expressions
grade_6.subject: math

grade_6.name: Equations and inequalities
grade_6.description: Understand solving equations and inequalities; substitute values to check solutions
grade_6.subject: math

grade_6.name: Statistical questions and data
grade_6.description: Recognize statistical questions; describe data distributions using mean, median, mode, range
grade_6.subject: math

grade_7.name: Proportional relationships
grade_7.description: Recognize and represent proportional relationships; compute unit rates with fractions
grade_7.subject: math

grade_7.name: Operations with integers
grade_7.description: Add, subtract, multiply, divide rational numbers including negative numbers
grade_7.subject: math

grade_7.name: Linear expressions and equations
grade_7.description: Apply properties to add, subtract, factor, expand linear expressions; solve linear equations
grade_7.subject: math

grade_7.name: Solving real-world problems with equations
grade_7.description: Solve word problems leading to linear equations and inequalities
grade_7.subject: math

grade_7.name: Scale drawings
grade_7.description: Solve problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures
grade_7.subject: math

grade_7.name: Area and circumference of circles
grade_7.description: Know and use formulas for area and circumference of circles
grade_7.subject: math

grade_7.name: Sampling and statistical inference
grade_7.description: Understand random sampling; use data from samples to draw inferences about populations
grade_7.subject: math

grade_7.name: Introduction to probability
grade_7.description: Understand probability of a chance event; approximate probability through experiments
grade_7.subject: math

grade_8.name: Rational and irrational numbers
grade_8.description: Understand and classify rational vs irrational numbers; approximate irrational numbers
grade_8.subject: math

grade_8.name: Properties of exponents
grade_8.description: Know and apply properties of integer exponents to generate equivalent expressions
grade_8.subject: math

grade_8.name: Scientific notation
grade_8.description: Use numbers expressed in scientific notation; compare and perform operations
grade_8.subject: math

grade_8.name: Linear relationships and slope
grade_8.description: Graph proportional relationships; interpret unit rate as slope; compare relationships
grade_8.subject: math

grade_8.name: Pythagorean theorem
grade_8.description: Apply Pythagorean theorem to find side lengths; determine right triangles
grade_8.subject: math

grade_8.name: Solving linear equations
grade_8.description: Solve linear equations in one variable including equations with rational coefficients
grade_8.subject: math

grade_8.name: Scatter plots and bivariate data
grade_8.description: Construct and interpret scatter plots; describe clustering, outliers, association
grade_8.subject: math

grade_1.name: What is sound?
grade_1.description: Sound is a form of energy; all sounds are made by vibrating objects
grade_1.subject: physics

grade_1.name: Vibrations make sound
grade_1.description: Vibrating objects produce sound; faster vibrations make higher-pitched sounds
grade_1.subject: physics

grade_1.name: Light and seeing
grade_1.description: We see objects because light travels from a source, hits objects, and enters our eyes
grade_1.subject: physics

grade_1.name: Light sources and darkness
grade_1.description: Without a light source, objects cannot be seen; shadows form when light is blocked
grade_1.subject: physics

grade_1.name: Reflection of light
grade_1.description: Light bounces off mirrors and shiny surfaces; the angle of reflection equals the angle of incidence
grade_1.subject: physics

grade_1.name: Using light to communicate
grade_1.description: Mirrors and reflected light can send signals; examples include lighthouses and mirrors
grade_1.subject: physics

grade_2.name: Pushes and pulls
grade_2.description: Objects at rest stay still until a push or pull acts on them; direction of force matters
grade_2.subject: physics

grade_2.name: Strength of force and motion
grade_2.description: A stronger push or pull changes an object's speed or direction more than a weaker one
grade_2.subject: physics

grade_2.name: Observable properties of materials
grade_2.description: Materials have properties: color, hardness, flexibility, texture, absorbency
grade_2.subject: physics

grade_2.name: Choosing materials for a purpose
grade_2.description: Different materials are chosen based on their properties to solve specific problems
grade_2.subject: physics

grade_3.name: Balanced and unbalanced forces
grade_3.description: When forces are balanced, objects stay still or move at constant speed; unbalanced forces change motion
grade_3.subject: physics

grade_3.name: How forces change motion
grade_3.description: Objects speed up, slow down, or change direction when an unbalanced force acts on them
grade_3.subject: physics

grade_3.name: Magnetic attraction and repulsion
grade_3.description: Magnets attract or repel each other; like poles repel, unlike poles attract
grade_3.subject: physics

grade_3.name: Forces at a distance
grade_3.description: Magnetic and gravitational forces act without direct contact; strength changes with distance
grade_3.subject: physics

grade_3.name: Gravity pulls objects down
grade_3.description: Earth's gravity pulls all objects toward the ground regardless of their mass
grade_3.subject: physics

grade_3.name: Friction as a contact force
grade_3.description: Friction is a force that resists motion between surfaces in contact; it can slow objects down
grade_3.subject: physics

grade_4.name: Forms of energy
grade_4.description: Energy exists in many forms: light, heat, sound, electrical, chemical, and mechanical
grade_4.subject: physics

grade_4.name: Energy transfer
grade_4.description: Energy can be transferred from one object to another through collisions, light, electricity, or heat
grade_4.subject: physics

grade_4.name: Kinetic energy
grade_4.description: Moving objects have kinetic energy; the faster and heavier an object, the more kinetic energy it has
grade_4.subject: physics

grade_4.name: Potential energy
grade_4.description: Stored energy depends on position or condition; higher objects have more gravitational potential energy
grade_4.subject: physics

grade_4.name: What are waves?
grade_4.description: Waves are repeating patterns of motion; they carry energy without carrying matter
grade_4.subject: physics

grade_4.name: Wave properties: amplitude and wavelength
grade_4.description: Amplitude is wave height (energy); wavelength is distance between peaks; frequency is waves per second
grade_4.subject: physics

grade_5.name: Gravity on Earth
grade_5.description: Gravity pulls all objects toward Earth's center; strength depends on mass of objects and distance
grade_5.subject: physics

grade_5.name: Gravity and orbits
grade_5.description: Gravity keeps planets in orbit around the sun and the moon in orbit around Earth
grade_5.subject: physics

grade_5.name: How light interacts with matter
grade_5.description: Light can be transmitted, reflected, or absorbed depending on the material it hits
grade_5.subject: physics

grade_5.name: Transparent, translucent, and opaque materials
grade_5.description: Materials are classified by how much light passes through them; this determines what we see
grade_5.subject: physics

grade_5.name: Food as chemical energy
grade_5.description: Food stores chemical energy that organisms release through digestion and respiration
grade_5.subject: physics

grade_5.name: Energy flow in ecosystems
grade_5.description: Energy from the sun flows through ecosystems: producers → consumers → decomposers
grade_5.subject: physics

grade_6.name: Newton's First Law — inertia
grade_6.description: Objects at rest stay at rest and objects in motion stay in motion unless acted on by a force
grade_6.subject: physics

grade_6.name: Newton's Second Law — F = ma
grade_6.description: Force equals mass times acceleration; greater force = greater acceleration; greater mass = less acceleration
grade_6.subject: physics

grade_6.name: Newton's Third Law — action and reaction
grade_6.description: For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction; forces always come in pairs
grade_6.subject: physics

grade_6.name: Wave speed, frequency, and wavelength
grade_6.description: Wave speed = frequency × wavelength; these properties are mathematically related
grade_6.subject: physics

grade_6.name: Sound waves and pitch
grade_6.description: Sound is a longitudinal wave; pitch depends on frequency; loudness depends on amplitude
grade_6.subject: physics

grade_6.name: Kinetic energy and mass formula
grade_6.description: KE = ½mv²; kinetic energy increases with mass and with the square of speed
grade_6.subject: physics

grade_6.name: Gravitational potential energy
grade_6.description: GPE = mgh; potential energy depends on mass, gravity, and height above the ground
grade_6.subject: physics

grade_7.name: Gravitational force and mass
grade_7.description: Gravitational force increases with mass; every object with mass attracts every other object
grade_7.subject: physics

grade_7.name: Electrostatic force
grade_7.description: Like charges repel, opposite charges attract; force weakens with distance (inverse square law)
grade_7.subject: physics

grade_7.name: The electromagnetic spectrum
grade_7.description: EM waves range from radio waves to gamma rays; all travel at the speed of light in a vacuum
grade_7.subject: physics

grade_7.name: Applications of the EM spectrum
grade_7.description: Different EM waves have different uses: radio for communication, X-rays for imaging, UV for sterilization
grade_7.subject: physics

grade_7.name: Energy transformation examples
grade_7.description: Energy converts between forms: chemical → kinetic in a car, electrical → light in a bulb
grade_7.subject: physics

grade_7.name: Conservation of energy
grade_7.description: Total energy in a closed system is always conserved; energy lost to heat is still energy
grade_7.subject: physics

grade_8.name: Electric fields and current
grade_8.description: Moving electric charges create current; electric fields exert forces on other charges
grade_8.subject: physics

grade_8.name: Magnetic fields and electromagnets
grade_8.description: Moving charges create magnetic fields; changing magnetic fields induce electric current
grade_8.subject: physics

grade_8.name: Analog vs. digital signals
grade_8.description: Analog signals are continuous; digital signals use binary (0s and 1s); digital is more reliable over distance
grade_8.subject: physics

grade_8.name: Information storage and transmission
grade_8.description: Digital information can be stored, copied, and transmitted without degradation; applications in technology
grade_8.subject: physics

grade_8.name: Energy in collisions
grade_8.description: In collisions, total energy is conserved; kinetic energy may convert to heat, sound, or deformation
grade_8.subject: physics

grade_8.name: Applying conservation of energy
grade_8.description: Apply energy conservation to real scenarios: roller coasters, pendulums, bouncing balls
grade_8.subject: physics

grade_2.name: Describing materials by properties
grade_2.description: Materials can be described and sorted by observable properties: color, texture, hardness, flexibility
grade_2.subject: chemistry

grade_2.name: Using properties to identify materials
grade_2.description: Each material has a unique set of properties; properties help us identify and choose materials
grade_2.subject: chemistry

grade_2.name: Heating changes materials
grade_2.description: Heating materials can change their properties: ice melts, bread toasts, clay hardens
grade_2.subject: chemistry

grade_2.name: Reversible vs. irreversible changes
grade_2.description: Some changes from heating/cooling can be reversed (melting ice); others cannot (burning wood)
grade_2.subject: chemistry

grade_2.name: Mixing materials together
grade_2.description: When materials are combined, they may keep their own properties or create new ones
grade_2.subject: chemistry

grade_2.name: New materials from mixing
grade_2.description: Some material combinations create new substances with different properties than the originals
grade_2.subject: chemistry

grade_3.name: Reversible physical changes
grade_3.description: Melting, freezing, dissolving, and evaporating are reversible physical changes
grade_3.subject: chemistry

grade_3.name: Irreversible chemical changes
grade_3.description: Burning, rusting, and cooking produce new substances; these changes cannot be reversed
grade_3.subject: chemistry

grade_4.name: Everything is made of matter
grade_4.description: All objects are made of matter; matter takes up space and has mass
grade_4.subject: chemistry

grade_4.name: Particles too small to see
grade_4.description: Matter is made of tiny particles; particle arrangement explains observable properties
grade_4.subject: chemistry

grade_5.name: Atoms and molecules
grade_5.description: All matter is made of atoms; atoms combine to form molecules; different atoms = different elements
grade_5.subject: chemistry

grade_5.name: Elements and the periodic table (intro)
grade_5.description: Elements are pure substances made of one type of atom; the periodic table organizes all known elements
grade_5.subject: chemistry

grade_5.name: Solids, liquids, and gases
grade_5.description: Matter exists in three common states; each has distinct particle arrangement and movement
grade_5.subject: chemistry

grade_5.name: Changes of state
grade_5.description: Matter changes state when heated or cooled: melting, freezing, evaporation, condensation, sublimation
grade_5.subject: chemistry

grade_5.name: Mixtures
grade_5.description: A mixture contains two or more substances that are physically combined but not chemically joined
grade_5.subject: chemistry

grade_5.name: Solutions and dissolving
grade_5.description: A solution forms when a solute dissolves in a solvent; dissolving is mixing at particle level
grade_5.subject: chemistry

grade_5.name: Separating mixtures
grade_5.description: Mixtures can be separated using physical properties: filtration, evaporation, magnetism, density
grade_5.subject: chemistry

grade_5.name: Mass is conserved in physical changes
grade_5.description: When matter changes state or is mixed, the total mass stays the same
grade_5.subject: chemistry

grade_5.name: Mass is conserved in chemical changes
grade_5.description: Even when new substances form in a chemical reaction, the total mass of reactants equals products
grade_5.subject: chemistry

grade_6.name: Atomic structure
grade_6.description: Atoms have a nucleus containing protons and neutrons, surrounded by electrons; atomic number defines elements
grade_6.subject: chemistry

grade_6.name: The periodic table
grade_6.description: Elements in the periodic table are organized by atomic number; rows are periods, columns are groups with similar properties
grade_6.subject: chemistry

grade_6.name: Signs of a chemical reaction
grade_6.description: Evidence of chemical reactions: color change, gas production, temperature change, precipitate formation
grade_6.subject: chemistry

grade_6.name: Reactants and products
grade_6.description: In a chemical reaction, reactants are consumed and products are formed; properties of products differ from reactants
grade_6.subject: chemistry

grade_6.name: Temperature and particle motion
grade_6.description: Temperature measures the average kinetic energy of particles; higher temperature = faster particle movement
grade_6.subject: chemistry

grade_6.name: Heat transfer and changes of state
grade_6.description: Adding or removing heat energy causes changes of state; boiling/melting points are characteristic properties
grade_6.subject: chemistry

grade_7.name: Atoms rearrange in reactions
grade_7.description: Chemical reactions involve breaking and forming bonds; atoms are rearranged but not created or destroyed
grade_7.subject: chemistry

grade_7.name: Balancing chemical equations
grade_7.description: Chemical equations must be balanced; the number of each type of atom is the same on both sides
grade_7.subject: chemistry

grade_7.name: Exothermic reactions
grade_7.description: Exothermic reactions release energy as heat or light; products have less energy than reactants
grade_7.subject: chemistry

grade_7.name: Endothermic reactions
grade_7.description: Endothermic reactions absorb energy from the surroundings; products have more energy than reactants
grade_7.subject: chemistry

grade_8.name: Polymers and synthetic materials
grade_8.description: Polymers are large molecules made of repeating units; plastics, nylon, and rubber are synthetic polymers
grade_8.subject: chemistry

grade_8.name: Designing materials for a purpose
grade_8.description: Scientists design new materials with specific properties by modifying molecular structure
grade_8.subject: chemistry

grade_8.name: Chemical energy in fuels
grade_8.description: Fuels store chemical energy; combustion releases this energy as heat and light
grade_8.subject: chemistry

grade_8.name: Chemical energy in food and biology
grade_8.description: Cellular respiration releases chemical energy from glucose; connects chemistry to life science
grade_8.subject: chemistry

grade_1.name: The sun's daily pattern
grade_1.description: The sun appears to rise in the east and set in the west each day due to Earth's rotation
grade_1.subject: earth_science

grade_1.name: Moon and star patterns
grade_1.description: The moon appears in different shapes (phases) over a month; stars appear to move across the night sky
grade_1.subject: earth_science

grade_1.name: Seasonal changes in daylight
grade_1.description: The number of daylight hours changes with the seasons; summer has longer days, winter has shorter days
grade_1.subject: earth_science

grade_1.name: Seasonal temperature and organism behavior
grade_1.description: Temperature changes seasonally; animals and plants respond to these changes
grade_1.subject: earth_science

grade_2.name: Wind and water shape Earth
grade_2.description: Wind and water slowly wear away rock and soil and deposit it elsewhere over long time periods
grade_2.subject: earth_science

grade_2.name: How humans change the landscape
grade_2.description: Humans modify Earth's surface by building, mining, farming, and planting; some changes help, some harm
grade_2.subject: earth_science

grade_2.name: Where is water on Earth?
grade_2.description: Water is found in oceans (saltwater), rivers, lakes (freshwater), and as ice in glaciers and polar regions
grade_2.subject: earth_science

grade_2.name: The water cycle basics
grade_2.description: Water evaporates from surfaces, forms clouds, and falls back as rain or snow — constantly cycling
grade_2.subject: earth_science

grade_3.name: Weather vs. climate
grade_3.description: Weather is day-to-day conditions; climate is the long-term average weather pattern for a region
grade_3.subject: earth_science

grade_3.name: Climate patterns across regions
grade_3.description: Different regions have different climates; scientists use data over many years to describe climate
grade_3.subject: earth_science

grade_3.name: Earthquakes and volcanoes
grade_3.description: Earthquakes and volcanic eruptions are caused by movements of Earth's crust; they can be destructive
grade_3.subject: earth_science

grade_3.name: Preparing for natural hazards
grade_3.description: Communities use science to predict, prepare for, and reduce the impact of natural hazards
grade_3.subject: earth_science

grade_4.name: Weathering breaks down rock
grade_4.description: Physical and chemical weathering breaks rock into smaller pieces over time; creates soil
grade_4.subject: earth_science

grade_4.name: Erosion and deposition
grade_4.description: Erosion moves weathered material; deposition drops it in a new location, building landforms
grade_4.subject: earth_science

grade_4.name: Earth's renewable resources
grade_4.description: Renewable resources (sunlight, wind, water, plants) replenish naturally and can be used sustainably
grade_4.subject: earth_science

grade_4.name: Earth's non-renewable resources
grade_4.description: Fossil fuels and minerals form over millions of years; once used, they are gone; conservation matters
grade_4.subject: earth_science

grade_5.name: Earth's four systems
grade_5.description: Earth has four interacting systems: geosphere (rock), hydrosphere (water), atmosphere (air), biosphere (life)
grade_5.subject: earth_science

grade_5.name: How Earth's systems interact
grade_5.description: Events in one system affect others: volcanic eruptions change the atmosphere, rain shapes the geosphere
grade_5.subject: earth_science

grade_5.name: Human activities and Earth
grade_5.description: Human activities such as deforestation, pollution, and burning fossil fuels affect Earth's systems
grade_5.subject: earth_science

grade_5.name: Solutions to protect Earth
grade_5.description: Humans can reduce their impact: recycling, renewable energy, conservation, and land protection
grade_5.subject: earth_science

grade_6.name: Plate tectonics
grade_6.description: Earth's crust is divided into tectonic plates that move slowly; their movement causes earthquakes and volcanoes
grade_6.subject: earth_science

grade_6.name: The rock cycle
grade_6.description: Rocks transform between igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic types through heat, pressure, and erosion
grade_6.subject: earth_science

grade_6.name: Evidence for climate change
grade_6.description: Data from ice cores, sea levels, temperature records, and glaciers show Earth is warming
grade_6.subject: earth_science

grade_6.name: Causes of climate change
grade_6.description: Rising CO₂ and greenhouse gases from human activity trap heat; this drives global warming
grade_6.subject: earth_science

grade_7.name: Geologic time scale
grade_7.description: Earth's 4.6-billion-year history is divided into eons, eras, periods; events are traced through rock layers
grade_7.subject: earth_science

grade_7.name: Fossils and evolution evidence
grade_7.description: Fossils provide evidence of past life; the fossil record shows how organisms changed over time
grade_7.subject: earth_science

grade_7.name: Impacts of climate change
grade_7.description: Climate change causes sea level rise, extreme weather events, habitat loss, and species extinction
grade_7.subject: earth_science

grade_7.name: Evaluating climate solutions
grade_7.description: Evaluate trade-offs of solutions: renewable energy, carbon capture, conservation policies
grade_7.subject: earth_science

grade_8.name: The water cycle in depth
grade_8.description: Water cycles through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and runoff; groundwater and aquifers
grade_8.subject: earth_science

grade_8.name: Ocean currents and climate
grade_8.description: Ocean currents distribute heat globally; thermohaline circulation affects regional climates
grade_8.subject: earth_science

grade_8.name: Resource use and sustainability
